Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function shows mildly elevated creatinine and hyperchloremia, suggesting possible mild renal impairment or dehydration. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Marked glycosuria and low HbA1c may indicate recent hypoglycemia or analytical error. Recommend clinical correlation for renal and metabolic status, and further evaluation of urine findings.
